data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S2 70c7: Content-addressed snapshot cache + background janitor)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). This child is the cache/janitor slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (materialize core: faithful read-tree+checkout-index, atomic population) + S3 (gate re-rooting onto the snapshot). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child: it CACHES the materialized entry but does not own materialization (its Scope: "Out: materialization itself (S1)"; its What: "populated via S1's atomic dir-rename"). Not an absence (S1/S3 cover it).
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by S4 (record+sign+in-toto) atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. This child REINFORCES reproducibility by making the immutable SHA a content-addressed, no-staleness cache key (a cache HIT serves byte-identical content), but does not sign (its Scope: "Out: ... verdict memoization"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — covered by S3 (in-place read) + S4 (signing bar). Local bypasses the cache entirely (S3: "no materialization, no cache"). This child claims nothing here. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; no worktrees; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the single-flight + delete-on-last-close half — its AC1 (two concurrent gates on the same SHA trigger exactly one materialization; the second reads the shared entry), its AC2 (evicting an entry under an open fd does not disrupt the in-flight read; new lookups cleanly miss and re-materialize), its AC10 (no PID/heartbeat lease; liveness rests on fds/flock). S1 owns the atomic-population + no-worktree + no index.lock/config.lock-contention half (S1 AC3/AC6). No double-implementation; no gap.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the SOLE OWNER and heart of the slice: its AC3 (janitor reclaims under a free-space watermark using LRU by touch-on-read mtime NOT atime, skipping the grace window, off the hot path), AC4 (secondary max-age cold-trim), AC8 (eviction is rename-to-trash then rmtree; no in-place recursive delete of a live entry), AC9 (a second process cannot run a GC pass concurrently — exclusive gc/lock; population remains lock-free), and AC2 (delete-on-last-close so eviction under an open fd does not disrupt the in-flight read) jointly deliver "reclaim disk under pressure WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads." Supporting ACs (AC5 startup sweep, AC6 byte-total no-TOCTOU-drift, AC7 trash-straggler re-drain, AC11 configurable tunables, AC12 ADR) harden this same slice. Clean.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises the actionable error; its AC5) + S6 (documents message + remedy + fail-closed/local-still-runs; its AC1/AC2). The fetch is S1's coalesced step; this child claims none. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (implementation: pure-Python tar-free extraction, gettempdir/mkdtemp, fcntl.flock + atomic-mkdir fallback; its AC4) + S6 (docs: REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + EFS/NFS flock caveat; its AC5). This child's cross-process pieces (optional flock(LOCK_EX) on locks/<sha>, exclusive flock on gc/lock, POSIX delete-on-last-close) RIDE the portability seam S1 establishes but do not re-own the tar/tmp/flock-fallback sub-clauses. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #2 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot of the project at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT USE by a distributed team; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; the unsigned local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC5/AC7. The "SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT USE" clause — specifically reclaiming disk WITHOUT corrupting concurrent readers — is precisely this child's core deliverable (its AC1-AC10), atop the immutable-SHA content-addressed cache key (no staleness) that makes "reproducible snapshot" hold across cache hits. The "signed/reproducible/verified_at_sha" and "unsigned local" clauses are carried by S4/S3. No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- This child's What explicitly delegates materialization to S1 ("populated via S1's atomic dir-rename"; "Out: materialization itself (S1)") and defers verdict memoization (fast-follow, also Out of parent scope). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling (S1) or out of epic scope. Covered-by-named-consumer.
- This child's ADR (its AC12) captures the cache/janitor architecture + the rejected PID+heartbeat lease (the spike outcome); the epic plan-review explicitly assigned the snapshot/cache/signing ADR cross-cutting across S2/S3/S4, with S2 authoring and S3/S4 cross-referencing — consistent, no unclaimed ADR obligation.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) about routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check is a CONSISTENCY (G4) seam between S3 and S4 — not a G3 union-coverage gap, and unrelated to this cache/janitor child. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, ref-coherence) remain CLAIMED by children, so the union covers them. G3 union-coverage of the parent criteria is complete.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the cache/janitor slice (content-addressed SHA-keyed cache, single-flight, delete-on-last-close reader safety, free-space-watermark LRU reclamation by touch-on-read mtime, max-age cold-trim, rename-to-trash eviction, gc/lock interlock, byte-total no-TOCTOU integrity, trash-straggler re-drain, startup sweep, self-heal, configurable tunables, ADR) of the parent criteria — it is the SOLE OWNER of parent AC5 and a co-owner of the concurrency half of AC4 (single-flight + delete-on-last-close + no-PID-lease). No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
